questions.explanation: Yesterday indicates past tense. 'Went' is the past tense of 'go'.
Use 'since' with a specific point in time (2020).

questions.prompt: If I ___ rich, I would travel the world.
questions.explanation: Second conditional uses 'were' for all subjects in the if-clause.

questions.prompt: There ___ many students in the classroom.
Despite the bad weather, they decided to ___ with the picnic.
questions.explanation: 'Go ahead' means to proceed with something. 'Carry on' means continue doing something.
Present simple negative uses 'doesn't' for third person singular.

questions.prompt: By the time you arrive, I ___ my homework.
questions.explanation: Future perfect tense for action completed before a future time.

questions.prompt: The new policy, ___ was implemented last month, has caused controversy.
I look forward to ___ from you.
questions.explanation: 'Look forward to' is followed by a gerund (-ing form).
